claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are there?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I'm responsible for one child. My son Alex is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Is Alex still in approved education or training (such as school, college, or an approved training program)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to ask about your living situation:

Do you live in the UK, and does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester. And yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Do you have the right to reside in the UK? For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen, so I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (his college A-levels), which means you can continue to receive Child Benefit even though he's over 16
- Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 who stay in approved education or training
- You live in the UK and are a UK citizen with the right to reside
- Alex lives with you, so you're responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You should be aware of this when claiming.

Also, remember that Child Benefit will stop if Alex:
- Leaves approved education or training
- Starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week
- Starts an apprenticeship
- Starts claiming certain benefits in his own right

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If Alex's circumstances change, make sure to notify HMRC.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is still in approved education (college doing A-levels). Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify if the child is under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is a UK citizen, so has the right to reside. Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 17-year-old son Alex, who is in approved education (college A-levels). Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility. No one else is claiming for Alex.

The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is a UK citizen, so has the right to reside. Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility. No one else is claiming for Alex.
